source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 8.1.1-8.1.2 device naming (pp. 127-128)

questions.q: How does a SCSI, SATA or USB hard disk appear under /dev?
questions.why: SCSI/SATA/USB disks are /dev/sd[a-z]; old IDE disks were /dev/hd[a-d].

questions.q: How does a VIRTUAL hard disk (as in a VM) typically appear?
Which service is responsible for applying the device naming rules to detected hardware?
questions.why: udev applies the naming conventions (rules) to the devices it detects.

questions.q: In /dev/sda3, what does the trailing number 3 mean?
options: It is the partition number on the block device sda
It is the number of sectors
questions.why: The number after the block device identifies a partition (here the 3rd on sda).

questions.q: Which device file name usually refers to the optical (CD/DVD) drive?
questions.why: The optical drive appears as /dev/cdrom or /dev/sr0; /dev/fd[0-7] is a floppy and /dev/lp[0-2] a parallel printer.
